title: crepis pygmaea
intro: Crepis pygmaea, also known as Dwarf Hawksbeard, is a small flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe, North Africa, and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crepis pygmaea include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic agent. Crepis pygmaea is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used as a tincture or added to salves and creams for topical application.
